task_completion.description: Permanent openings are created in the Wall object using the cutters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Blender 3D viewport, the solid 'Wall' object should visibly have three distinct holes cut into it (two window-sized holes and one door-sized hole at the bottom).

task_completion.description: The red cutter objects are no longer visible in the viewport or render.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The red objects ('WindowCutter_Left', 'WindowCutter_Right', 'DoorCutter') are completely absent from the 3D viewport. In the Outliner panel, they should either be deleted or have their visibility (eye icon) and render (camera icon) toggled off.

task_completion.description: The scene is rendered from the current camera view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Blender Render' window is open and displays the final image of the wall with the three holes. The red cutters must not be visible in this rendered image.

task_completion.description: The rendered image is saved to the expected path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender's 'Save Image' dialog shows the file being saved as 'wall_render.png' in '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/', or a file manager/terminal shows this file exists.

task_completion.description: The Blender project is saved to the expected path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender application title bar displays 'wall_with_openings.blend', or the 'Save As' dialog shows it being saved to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/wall_with_openings.blend'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Blender GUI to perform the boolean operations and rendering.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating Blender's Modifier Properties tab, Outliner, and Render menus, rather than executing a Python script in the terminal to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The render was actually executed by the Blender engine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender Render window should appear showing the progressive or finished render of the specific scene, proving the image wasn't just downloaded or copied.

integrity.description: The output files were saved via Blender and not mocked.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for Blender's file browser dialogs or status bar confirmation messages for saving the .blend and .png files, ensuring the agent didn't just use 'touch' in the terminal to create empty files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Wall object starts as a 6-face cube. After successfully applying the boolean difference modifiers using the three cutters ('WindowCutter_Left', 'WindowCutter_Right', 'DoorCutter'), the Wall should have at least 15 faces. The final render must be saved to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/wall_render.png' and the project to '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/wall_with_openings.blend'. The cutters must not be visible in the final viewport or render.
